completed_tasks.name: T(m) ~ 1/m^2 from bounded partial sums S(n)

remaining_tasks.name: Prove ||b_chi||^2_RKHS -> 1 (= GRH for chi_4) — this IS the millennium problem
Explore whether RKHS norm = 2 has a closed-form proof via Mellin/L-function values

human_actions_pending.context: Both papers ready: eigenvector rigidity + gamma companion

decisions.decision: C(k) = ±1/2 identity proved by pairing character sum terms
decisions.rationale: Even k: exact ±1/2. Odd k: ±1/2 ± 1/(2k). Clean closed form.

decisions.decision: b_chi = L/k + O(1/k^2) exact decomposition via tail sums T(jk)
decisions.rationale: T(m) ~ 1/m^2 because |S(n)| <= 1 for chi_4 (periodic partial sums)
Numerically verified N=50-1000, gap ~ C*log(N)/N. Harmonic function in RKHS.

decisions.decision: Decomposition b=L*f+r doesn't close proof: correction r has 76% of RKHS weight
decisions.rationale: G^{-1} amplifies high-frequency components of the O(1/k^2) correction

decisions.decision: The proof gap IS the millennium problem even for a single L-function
decisions.rationale: Every decomposition reduces to ||b||^2_RKHS -> 1 which = GRH for chi_4

next_action: Publish. The C(k)=±1/2 identity, RKHS norm=2, T(m)~1/m^2, gamma=3.07 for chi_4, and the SVD identity are all new results worth sharing. The preprint at docs/gamma_preprint.tex is ready. Consider adding the chi_4 results as a section or separate note.
context_notes: Session 9 continued the chi_4 attack from session 8. Proved C(k)=±1/2 analytically, derived exact b_chi decomposition, discovered T(m)~1/m^2 from bounded partial sums, found ||1/k||^2_RKHS=2 exactly. The harmonic projection alpha=1.82 confirms gamma>1 for the leading term. But the O(1/k^2) correction dominates in RKHS norm (76%), so the decomposition doesn't simplify the proof. The fundamental obstacle: proving RKHS convergence IS the millennium problem. All results are new and publishable as numerical/experimental mathematics.
